dwelling_habits.dwelling: Open tropical oceans — floatsam lines, weed patches, and buoy shadows. Rarely a beach tourist; you’ll meet them farther offshore or on a plate.
dwelling_habits.food: Fast hunters of flying fish, squid, and whatever smaller fish hide under floating debris.
dwelling_habits.feeding: Accelerates like a sports car — chase, slash, gulp. Flashy gold-green colors intensify when they hunt or fight.
dwelling_habits.mating: Grows up shockingly fast and breeds often; big males (bulls) sport a high blunt forehead that kids compare to a swimming doorstop.
dwelling_habits.socializing: Often travel in pairs or small groups under the same bit of debris — roommates with fins.

fun_facts: “Mahi mahi” means “strong-strong” in Hawaiian — and they live up to the double name with double attitude.
They change colors like mood rings when excited… which is most of the time they’re around anglers.
